patch.value: MCP pm_create/pm_update crashed with 'raw.trim is not a function' when priority sent as JSON number

Pre-existing bug surfaced by dogfood 2026-05-28: MCP clients (including Claude's MCP tool calls) JSON-encode priority as a native number (e.g. priority: 3). The CLI's resolvePriority(raw: string) called raw.trim() unconditionally, throwing TypeError before the priority was ever interpreted. Direct CLI calls (which always pass --priority 3 as a string) were unaffected. Fix: widened resolvePriority to accept string | number, with Number.isInteger + 0..4 range check for the number branch. ensurePriority in create.ts and update.ts widened accordingly. Test added in tests/unit/priority.spec.ts covering native-number happy-path, out-of-range / non-integer rejection, and defense against null/undefined/object/array/boolean.

Widened resolvePriority(raw: string | number) in src/core/item/priority.ts: native numbers in [0..4] integer range pass through; non-integer or out-of-range numbers throw the existing USAGE error. ensurePriority in create.ts and update.ts widened to match.
MCP tool calls with {priority: 3} (JSON number) succeed and create/update items normally.

Verified: stdio MCP test reproduced 'raw.trim is not a function' against old build, then succeeded after rebuild (different error: 'Tracker not initialized' for the unrelated temp path). New priority.spec.ts tests cover happy-path native numbers, rejection of non-integer / out-of-range numbers, and defense against null/undefined/object/array/boolean inputs. tsc --noEmit clean; existing 7 priority tests still green.
Shipped: resolvePriority now accepts string|number with strict integer-range check on the number branch.
